message:
chore(beads): close D2.1 with refreshed prepared-artifact inventory verifier

Close issue bd-db300.4.2.1 (D2.1: inventory clone drop and
rebuild-heavy prepared-artifact paths) after revalidation against
current code.

Verifier script updates (verify_bd_db300_4_2_1_prepared_artifact_inventory.sh):
- Update code references to match current API shapes:
  dml_statement: Some(Arc::new(...)) → PreparedDmlDispatch::Deferred(Arc::new(...))
  self.execute_statement_impl(dml.as_ref(), ...) → if let Some(dml) = stmt.deferred_dml_statement()
- Add REF_PREPARED_DML_FAST_PATH tracking the
  stmt.dispatch_precompiled_program().is_some() fast path
- Update inventory prose for ranks 1 and 4: compile_and_wrap() now
  sometimes reuses cached VdbePrograms, rank 4 reflects
  PreparedDmlDispatch::Deferred and fast-path vs deferred split

Beads metadata (.beads/issues.jsonl):
- Close bd-db300.4.2.1 with assignee FoggyHeron
- Add comment documenting the revalidation and verifier update

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Added quantified parser/AST churn, record decode, and row materialization cost components to HotPathActionableRanking plus markdown/script rendering so D2-D4 can rank from evidence-backed shares. Targeted rch validation in progress: hot_path_profile_smoke_writes_artifacts passed.","created_at":"2026-03-11T21:57:04Z"},{"id":2259,"issue_id":"bd-db300.4.1.2","author":"SilverForge","text":"Validation update: `rch exec -- cargo check -p fsqlite-e2e --all-targets` passed after the patch (only observed warning was a pre-existing dead_code warning in fsqlite-core::Connection::invalidate_compiled_cache). `rch exec -- cargo test -p fsqlite-e2e hot_path_profile_smoke_writes_artifacts -- --nocapture` passed. Lightweight follow-up checks also passed locally: `rustfmt +nightly --check crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/perf_runner.rs`, `git diff --check`, and `bash -n scripts/verify_bd_db300_4_1_hot_path_profiles.sh`. Full extra remote validation hit infrastructure faults: one worker lacked stdarg.h for libsqlite3-sys bindgen, and another rch sync raced on .git/modules/legacy_sqlite_code/sqlite/index.lock and fell back local, so I aborted rather than rely on non-offloaded execution.","created_at":"2026-03-11T22:06:32Z"},{"id":2268,"issue_id":"bd-db300.4.1.2","author":"IvoryLark","text":"Validation in progress for a local follow-up: tightened the hot-path artifact contract in crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/perf_runner.rs so D1.2 mapping invariants are asserted deterministically from a structured profile snapshot. Focused rch test passed; workspace rch cargo check passed; waiting on definitive workspace clippy result. Workspace cargo fmt --check is blocked by unrelated dirty formatting in crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs, but rustfmt +nightly --check passes for crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/perf_runner.rs.","created_at":"2026-03-12T11:37:07Z"}]}

Implemented the missing confidence side of the campaign hotspot ledger in scripts/verify_bd_db300_4_1_hot_path_profiles.sh: the aggregated actionable_ranking.json now uses schema fsqlite-e2e.hot_path_campaign_ranking.v2 and adds confidence_score_basis_points, confidence_label, confidence_rationale, plus run/mode/fixture coverage metadata for named_hotspots, allocator_pressure, and cost_components. The markdown summary now surfaces confidence next to the top hotspot/component entries, and the script validates those confidence fields before reporting success. Verification completed locally with bash -n, git diff --check, and an extracted jq smoke run against synthetic run_records.jsonl that produced the expected v2 ranking schema and confidence fields. I also launched a minimized real-path smoke campaign via the script with FIXTURE_IDS=frankensqlite_beads MODE_IDS=mvcc CONCURRENCY=2 SCALE=5 and a dedicated output dir; the remote rch build+hot-profile run completed successfully and emitted the inline bundle, but the top-level wrapper still appears to be hanging during post-run retrieval/finalization, so the campaign-level output directory is not fully finalized yet.","created_at":"2026-03-12T01:04:45Z"},{"id":2274,"issue_id":"bd-db300.4.1.3","author":"BlackHill","text":"Verification update: the refreshed smoke campaign under artifacts/perf/bd-db300.4.1/blackhill-smoke-3 finalized the previously hanging post-run wrapper and produced the reusable ranked hotspot ledger. actionable_ranking.json now includes confidence_label/confidence_score_basis_points plus explicit D2/D3/D4 bead mappings, and summary.md surfaces the same ranked execution-engine hotspots and quantified cost components for the canonical frankensqlite_beads MVCC hot cell. This satisfies the D1.3 publish-and-map contract.","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:31:36Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.4.2","title":"D2: cut parser, AST, and program clone-drop churn through reuse","description":"## Goal
Reduce parser, AST, and program clone/drop churn by reusing prepared artifacts where validity rules allow it. The point is to stop rebuilding or cloning execution metadata so often that allocator and drop costs become visible in a benchmark that is supposed to be about database work.

## Acceptance Criteria
- At least one meaningful clone/drop-heavy path is replaced with reuse or a cheaper representation.
- Correctness rules for invalidation and statement reuse are explicit.
- Measurement shows the targeted churn actually drops.
- Targeted unit tests are added or refreshed for the exact invariants and regressions touched by this bead.
- Where behavior crosses subsystem boundaries, canonical-matrix end-to-end validation with detailed structured logging is added or updated alongside the change.","acceptance_criteria":"- At least one meaningful clone/drop-heavy path is replaced with reuse or a cheaper representation.
- Correctness rules for invalidation and statement reuse are explicit.
- Measurement shows the targeted churn actually drops.
- Targeted unit tests are added or refreshed for the exact invariants and regressions touched by this bead.
- Where behavior crosses subsystem boundaries, canonical-matrix end-to-end validation with detailed structured logging is added or updated alongside the change.","notes":"This bead should not create backwards-compatibility shims or unclear caching rules. If the right answer is a cleaner prepared-artifact lifecycle, implement that directly.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T04:58:52.619016338Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-10T18:51:14.141034575Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["db300-program","db300-track-d","memory-management","parser","perf","planner","prepared-statements","track-d","vdbe"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:59Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-db300.4.2.3","title":"D2.3: verify invalidation correctness and churn reduction","description":"## Goal
verify invalidation correctness and churn reduction

## Checklist
1. Validate invalidation and statement-reuse correctness.
2. Measure the drop in clone or drop churn.
3. Package the result for later scorecards.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Validate invalidation and statement-reuse correctness.
2. Measure the drop in clone or drop churn.
3. Package the result for later scorecards.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Invalidation and statement-reuse correctness are verified under repeated execution, schema churn, and rollback/reset boundaries, not just in the steady-state happy path.
2. The bead measures whether clone/drop churn actually falls on representative rows and packages the result in a way later scorecards can attribute to the reuse work.
3. Verification includes enough logging/artifact detail that future agents can distinguish true churn reduction from workload drift.
4. The result is strong enough to support later reuse decisions without reopening old profiling sessions.","notes":"Verification obligations:
- validate invalidation and statement-reuse correctness under repeated execution, schema churn, and lifecycle-boundary cases
- measure clone/drop churn reduction on representative rows and package the result with clear provenance and row identity
- require enough logs/counters that later scorecard work can attribute wins or neutral outcomes to reuse rather than to unrelated workload changes
- optimize for users by making reuse claims auditable instead of requiring code archaeology to understand what actually improved","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T16:48:16.510349269Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-15T00:26:40.713427942Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","db300-program","db300-track-d","prepared-statements","track-d","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.4.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.4.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:59Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.4.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.4.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:59Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.4.3","title":"D3: add arena or scratch-space row decode and materialization buffers","description":"## Goal
Introduce arena- or scratch-based row decode and materialization buffers so hot query/update paths stop allocating and freeing short-lived objects at high frequency. This bead is about moving work from the general allocator onto bounded, reusable thread-local or execution-local storage.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Row decode/materialization reuses bounded scratch space or arenas.
- Allocator pressure drops in profiling on the targeted path.
- The ownership/reset rules are clear and covered by tests.
- Targeted unit tests are added or refreshed for the exact invariants and regressions touched by this bead.
- Where behavior crosses subsystem boundaries, canonical-matrix end-to-end validation with detailed structured logging is added or updated alongside the change.","acceptance_criteria":"- Row decode/materialization reuses bounded scratch space or arenas.
- Allocator pressure drops in profiling on the targeted path.
- The ownership/reset rules are clear and covered by tests.
- Targeted unit tests are added or refreshed for the exact invariants and regressions touched by this bead.
- Where behavior crosses subsystem boundaries, canonical-matrix end-to-end validation with detailed structured logging is added or updated alongside the change.","notes":"SmallVec, arenas, and execution-local scratch regions are all valid tools here if they fit the lifetime and concurrency model. The important thing is to kill repetitive short-lived heap churn.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T04:59:25.618749684Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-10T18:51:14.346137092Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["copy-reduction","db300-program","db300-track-d","decoding","memory-management","perf","track-d","vdbe"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:59Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.4.3.1","title":"D3.1: choose scratch or arena ownership model for decode and materialization","description":"## Goal
choose scratch or arena ownership model for decode and materialization

## Checklist
1. Choose the scratch or arena ownership model for decode paths.
2. Make reset rules and lifetimes explicit.
3. Keep the choice compatible with concurrency and executor lifetimes.

## Acceptance Criteria

@@ -156,9 +156,10 @@ REF_SCHEMA_INVALIDATION="$(find_line_ref "crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs"
 REF_COMPILE_CACHE="$(find_line_ref "crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs" 'fn compile_with_cache')"
 REF_REWRITE_SUBQUERY="$(find_line_ref "crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs" 'fn rewrite_subquery_statement')"
 REF_DEFERRED_QUERY_CLONE="$(find_line_ref "crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs" 'deferred_query_statement: Some\(Arc::new\(statement\.clone\(\)\)\)')"
-REF_DML_AST_CLONE="$(find_line_ref "crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs" 'dml_statement: Some\(Arc::new\(statement\.clone\(\)\)\)')"
+REF_DML_AST_CLONE="$(find_line_ref "crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs" 'PreparedDmlDispatch::Deferred\(Arc::new\(statement\.clone\(\)\)\)')"
+REF_PREPARED_DML_FAST_PATH="$(find_line_ref "crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs" 'if stmt\.dispatch_precompiled_program\(\)\.is_some\(\)')"
 REF_PREPARED_QUERY_REDISPATCH="$(find_line_ref "crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs" 'if let Some\(statement\) = &self\.deferred_query_statement')"
-REF_PREPARED_DML_REDISPATCH="$(find_line_ref "crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs" 'self\.execute_statement_impl\(dml\.as_ref\(\), p, stmt\.dispatch_precompiled_program\(\)\)\?')"
+REF_PREPARED_DML_REDISPATCH="$(find_line_ref "crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs" 'if let Some\(dml\) = stmt\.deferred_dml_statement\(\)')"
 REF_DISTINCT_LIMIT_CLONE="$(find_line_ref "crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs" 'let mut unbounded = select\.clone\(\)')"
 
 emit_event "scan_code" "pass" "pass" "resolved code references for prepared-artifact churn sites"
@@ -184,6 +185,7 @@ jq -n \
   --arg ref_rewrite_subquery "${REF_REWRITE_SUBQUERY}" \
   --arg ref_deferred_query_clone "${REF_DEFERRED_QUERY_CLONE}" \
   --arg ref_dml_ast_clone "${REF_DML_AST_CLONE}" \
+  --arg ref_prepared_dml_fast_path "${REF_PREPARED_DML_FAST_PATH}" \
   --arg ref_prepared_query_redispatch "${REF_PREPARED_QUERY_REDISPATCH}" \
   --arg ref_prepared_dml_redispatch "${REF_PREPARED_DML_REDISPATCH}" \
   --arg ref_distinct_limit_clone "${REF_DISTINCT_LIMIT_CLONE}" \
@@ -218,7 +220,7 @@ jq -n \
     inventory: [
       {
         rank: 1,
-        label: "prepare() canonicalizes SQL and compile_and_wrap() rebuilds prepared artifacts outside the compiled cache",
+        label: "prepare() canonicalizes SQL and compile_and_wrap() rebuilds prepared wrappers even when branch-local codegen reuses cached programs",
         code_ref: $ref_compile_and_wrap,
         supporting_refs: [
           $ref_prepare,
@@ -233,9 +235,9 @@ jq -n \
           parse_time_ns_total: $parser_metrics.parse_time_ns_total,
